text: Optical neural networks (ONNs) are considered as one of the feasible solutions for the implementation of fast and low power consumption artificial intelligence systems using the high parallel processing characteristics of light. However, the large-scale integration and high precision operation is still a major challenge for the realization of ONNs. In this paper, a fully integrated photonic processing unit (PPU) has been developed using an on-chip driven AlGaAs-on-insulator dark-pulse microcomb, and high speed parallel data processing is realized by utilizing a convolution operation based on time-wavelength plane stretching. The integrated analog photonics is highly sensitive, so a special control and calibration protocol was established, and a record weight precision of 9 bits was obtained. Thus, the system exhibits the maximum photonic-core compute density of 1.04 TOPS mm⁻², which is 5 times more than that of the wavelength-division multiplexing (WDM) ONNs, and the corresponding PPU is used for image edge detection and handwritten digit recognition, and the average classification accuracies of the corresponding digital computers are maintained. The highly scalable system promotes the parallel photonic accelerators from the laboratory development to the practical high performance applications, such as autonomous driving and real-time video recognition.
